ALL CASES CITED ADDRESS FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION ON A DEMURRER. PLAINTIFF DID NOT OBJECT TO THIS PROCEDURAL ISSUE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE: A. Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Gayle McKenzie's Motion To Strike Complaint is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fraud and request for punitive damages with requisite particularity. The Court notes that Defendant failed to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to allege fraud with particularity is subject to a motion to strike. All cases cited address fraud cause of action on a demurrer. Plaintiff did not object to this procedural issue. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GAYLE MCKENZIE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT to be heard in Department 501, at 9:30 a.m., on March 13, 2017. Judge Kahn has recused himself and Department 501 handles matters related to the injury to real property pursuant to SF Local Rule 8.10. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
